pages.full_text:
Sunday Simon + Eve picked up Tinder at 9.30 so only £15. Then we went to St. Fagan's museum. The main building opens later in the year. So we looked at out door buildings that have been moved there.
    A gorse mill - Small. 2 men + 1 boy wd. take 20 min. to process a bunch of gorse - This was fed to livestock. Cd. be fed all year + grew on worst soil, so better soil cd be used for other crops.
    We went to a pub for lunch + raced home (lots of traffic) + got there with 10 minutes to spare to get to church.
    __Thursday May 3rd__
    We set off at 9, voted (Newman House) for city councillors + dropped off Tinder at Kenads. Lunch in the van at Cobham services + on to Ightham Mote. Lovely [C4] house. Beautiful linen fold panelling, paint colour in rooms good, welcoming. Well worth a visit. V.beautiful.
    On to the Plough Inn Stalisfield, where Simon has already booked supper. They have put us in the fireplace again. They are building
pages.notes: The 'C4' or similar notation before 'house' may be a National Trust grade classification marker; could also read as 'NT' or a date. 'Kenads' could be a campsite or location name — spelling uncertain. Sunday entry continues from a previous page; date estimated as 2018-04-29 based on Thursday May 3rd 2018 being confirmed by the NT leaflet on the right page.

pages.full_text: holiday lets in the next field. It has been cold for days but now there is nice warm evening sunshine.
photo_captions: Ightham Mote — Kent 2018 — House, garden and estate (National Trust leaflet cover, glued in)
pages.notes: Page contains a circular Ightham Mote sticker/badge and a glued-in National Trust leaflet for Ightham Mote, Kent, dated 2018. The '2018' on the leaflet confirms the year. Handwritten text is a continuation from the left page describing the Plough Inn at Stalisfield.
